Title: Fed chair nominee Warsh may want smaller Fed holdings, but that's not easy to do

Sentence: More broadly, using the balance sheet as a tool has become a standard part of the monetary policy toolkit, and a critical one given increased probabilities of short-term rates being cut to near-zero ⁠levels in times of trouble.

Title: Snowflake partners with OpenAI in $200 million AI deal
Sentence: Feb 2 (Reuters) - Snowflake (SNOW.N), opens new tab said on Monday it has entered a $200 million partnership with OpenAI to integrate advanced artificial intelligence models directly into its cloud data platform, as enterprises increasingly turn to AI to extract insights from vast troves of data.

Title: Snowflake partners with OpenAI in $200 million AI deal
Sentence: Cloud data platforms, where companies store much of their most valuable and sensitive information, are emerging as a key battleground for generative AI businesses.

Sentence: The partnership reflects a broader shift among enterprises, which are moving from experimenting with basic AI chatbots to deploying integrated agents that can work directly with proprietary data, increasingly prioritizing systems that combine automation with tighter oversight.

Title: Snowflake partners with OpenAI in $200 million AI deal

Title: Football DailyRonaldo and an argument at Al-Nassr that doesn’t make sense
Sentence: For a man who is raging against the dying of the light, Cristiano Ronaldo is doing a good job of throwing a blanket over the fire.

Title: Football DailyRonaldo and an argument at Al-Nassr that doesn’t make sense
Sentence: And by throwing a blanket, what Football Daily really means to say is throwing a hissy fit, a wobbly, a tantrum.

Title: Football DailyRonaldo and an argument at Al-Nassr that doesn’t make sense
Sentence: The legendary footballer, reduced to something of a tap-in artist these days in a league of – at best – questionable quality, seemingly threw his toys out of his Bugatti on Sunday after deciding that not enough investment has been made in his club, Al-Nassr, in comparison to the other Saudi ones.

Title: Football DailyRonaldo and an argument at Al-Nassr that doesn’t make sense
Sentence: Ronaldo is reportedly unhappy because he feels Al-Ahli, Al-Ittihad, Al-Hilal, the three other clubs majority owned by the Public Investment Fund (PIF, the state-owned organisation funding much of Saudi sport) have received more financial backing than Al-Nassr.

Title: Football DailyRonaldo and an argument at Al-Nassr that doesn’t make sense
Sentence: Such is the ferocity of the latest huff by Ronaldo that the Portuguese, who turns 41 on Thursday, apparently boycotted Monday’s match against local rivals Al-Riyadh as he attempts to strongarm his bosses.

Title: Football DailyRonaldo and an argument at Al-Nassr that doesn’t make sense
Sentence: This was something of a disappointment for Al-Nassr and their fans, to be without their captain, leader, legend and top scorer for the crucial derby.

Title: Football DailyRonaldo and an argument at Al-Nassr that doesn’t make sense
Sentence: “His absence is not related to any physical issue nor to workload or fitness management,” parped Fabrizio Romano, seemingly a man in the know.

Title: Football DailyRonaldo and an argument at Al-Nassr that doesn’t make sense
Sentence: Football Daily can unfortunately see a few flaws in Ronaldo’s apparent arguments.

Title: Football DailyRonaldo and an argument at Al-Nassr that doesn’t make sense
Sentence: Firstly, plenty of money has already been spent by PIF on Al-Nassr.

Title: Football DailyRonaldo and an argument at Al-Nassr that doesn’t make sense
Sentence: This season only one PIF club (Al-Hilal) has spent more than Al-Nassr, who have signed some of Europe’s best players in Mohamed Simakan (from RB Leipzig, £30m), João Félix (from Chelsea, £26m) and Kingsley Coman (from Bayern Munich for £21.5m), all arriving to the tune of nearly £100m.

Title: Football DailyRonaldo and an argument at Al-Nassr that doesn’t make sense
Sentence: Secondly, Ronaldo might be part of the problem, with his weekly tax-free wage of £3.74m four times greater than the next-best paid player in the Saudi Pro League, Al-Ahli’s Riyad Mahrez (who takes home a paltry £870k every seven days).

Title: Football DailyRonaldo and an argument at Al-Nassr that doesn’t make sense
Sentence: Ronaldo’s apparent stance also seems somewhat self-defeating.

Title: Football DailyRonaldo and an argument at Al-Nassr that doesn’t make sense
Sentence: Not only is he making his first Saudi Pro League title less likely, but by not playing he would also be doing himself out of another opportunity to edge towards his stated aim of 1,000 career goals.

Title: Football DailyRonaldo and an argument at Al-Nassr that doesn’t make sense
Sentence: Ronaldo is currently stuck on 961 goals as he chases what would be an historic milestone.

Sentence: Those 39 goals aren’t going to score themselves, although in fairness PIF doesn’t know exactly what Ronaldo’s next set of demands are going to be.
